team.title: Softball — 2026 Bulldogs
team.name: Haxtun Bulldogs 9-12
team.blurb: Three McConnell girls suited up for the Bulldogs this summer.

tyndle.summary: Led the team in hits, tied for the RBI lead, and recorded the most assists. Walk-off RBI single vs Grant on 6/9 to win it 7-6.
tyndle.allStar: Named to the 2026 League All-Star team.

oakley.stats: 24 PA (most on team) · 12 walks (tied team-best) · 1.000 fielding
oakley.summary: The team's most patient at-bat and a perfect glove at second.

tayla.summary: Patrolled right field and showed up every time her number was called.

personal.title: Three sisters, CARA gymnastics
personal.text: Tyndle, Oakley, and Tayla with their CARA gymnastics ribbons after a meet.

events.name: Commerce City Meet

events.notes: Sessions: Compulsory 2 & 3 (7:00am), Optional 1 & 2 (11:00am), and Compulsory 4 / Optional 3 & 4 (3:15pm).

events.name: Carbon Valley Gymnastics Meet
events.host: Carbon Valley Parks and Recreation District
events.league: CARA — Colorado Association of Recreational Athletics
events.competitors: ~150–250 gymnasts (estimate, six full CARA teams across C3, C4, and Optionals 1–5)
events.notes: Spectator seating is on the upper level (running track). Limited chairs are provided — you may bring your own, for use on the upper level only.
events.photoCaption: Carbon Valley Recreation Center — competition floor seen from the upper-level seating, May 16, 2026.

memories.title: 2026 Bulldogs — All-Star season at shortstop
memories.text: Tyndle (#22) led the Haxtun Bulldogs 9-12 in hits and tied for the RBI lead from shortstop, also taking the circle as a pitcher and recording the team's most assists (.429 average, 9 hits, 6 RBI, 8 assists). Her signature moment: a walk-off RBI single against Grant on June 9 to win it 7-6. Named to the 2026 League All-Star team.

memories.title: 2026 Bulldogs — patient bat, perfect glove
memories.text: Oakley (#7) anchored second base and led the Bulldogs to the plate with 24 plate appearances, drawing 12 walks (tied for the team best) and fielding a flawless 1.000. The most patient at-bat in the lineup and a sure glove all summer.

memories.title: 2026 Bulldogs — steady in right field
memories.text: Tayla (#16) patrolled right field across 8 games, chipping in an RBI and 2 walks while fielding a clean 1.000. She showed up every time her number was called.

memories.title: Carbon Valley Gymnastics Meet — Optional 1
memories.text: Tyndle competed at Optional 1 in Frederick and placed 5th on Vault with a 7.5 — the same score that punched her regionals ticket. Beam was 5.4, Floor 6.6, and Bars 4.0 after a tough landing. All-Around 23.5. Walked out with a green CARA 5th-place Vault ribbon and a trip to regionals on the same event. First meet in the archive.
memories.photoCaption: Carbon Valley Recreation Center — meet day.

memories.title: Carbon Valley Gymnastics Meet — Level 3
memories.text: Oakley competed at Level 3 in Frederick and qualified for regionals on Floor with a 7.3. Vault was 6.0, Beam 6.5, Bars 6.3. All-Around 26.1. First meet in the archive, and the one with the regionals ticket on Floor.
memories.photoCaption: Carbon Valley Recreation Center — meet day.

memories.title: Carbon Valley Gymnastics Meet — Level 3
memories.text: Tayla competed at Level 3 in Frederick. Bars 6.9, Floor 7.0, Beam 6.5, Vault 5.4. All-Around 25.8. Beam was just a tenth shy of the regionals line and Floor was two-tenths off — both within reach next meet.
memories.photoCaption: Carbon Valley Recreation Center — meet day.
